title: pinaropappus spathulatus
intro: Pinaropappus spathulatus, also known as the desert chicory or desert scorpionweed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Pinaropappus spathulatus include its ability to aid in the healing of wounds, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of Pinaropappus spathulatus can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and it is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its effects.
